query: at what sustained traffic level does ExpressRoute become cheaper than site-to-site VPN — assume 500 Mbps baseline

metrics.content:
I’ll route this through the loaded network-desk pricing guidance and keep the answer text-only.

**ExpressRoute becomes cheaper at roughly ~15–25 Mbps sustained outbound traffic**, using Azure public-rate assumptions for VPN internet egress vs. ExpressRoute metered egress.

At a **500 Mbps sustained baseline**, ExpressRoute is already far cheaper:

| Option | Approx. monthly traffic | Approx. monthly cost driver |
|---|---:|---:|
| S2S VPN | ~164 TB/month | Internet egress dominates, often **>$10K/month** |
| ExpressRoute metered 500 Mbps | ~164 TB/month | Port + ~$0.025/GB, roughly **$4K–$5K/month plus gateway/provider fees** |
| ExpressRoute unlimited/local | ~164 TB/month | Usually even better if traffic is predictable |

**Bottom line:** if the 500 Mbps is sustained cloud-to-on-prem/outbound traffic, ExpressRoute is not near the break-even point — it is well past it. VPN only stays cheaper for low or bursty traffic, typically below a few tens of Mbps sustained.

> Analysis only — verify against vendor documentation before applying.
